title: Data Questions, Displays, Summaries, and Responsible Claims · Mastery Check
summary: Investigate service wait times and satisfaction while protecting the context behind the data. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a community-service data story. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Formulate a statistical question that anticipates variability and names a population.
Classify variables and evaluate whether a sample can represent the target population.
Choose tables and graphs whose encodings fit the variable and comparison.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-beginner cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper

# Add one tested model increment per activity.

steps.title: Reconstruct the defensible process
steps.instruction: Order the Statistical Question evidence cycle before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Statistical Question assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: Formulate a statistical question that anticipates variability and names a population. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Name the boundaries of the idea, compare a valid case with a near miss, and trace the causal difference.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Use a changed worked example case derived from this module: math beginner data statistics. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. A defensible solution applies Statistical Question, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a community-service data story.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: A question with one fixed answer is automatically statistical. This fails the Statistical Question contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.label: Research anchor
content.value: SIAM Modeling Across the Curriculum (2014 report) — Controls the modeling thread and changed-context project design.

content.value: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Formulate a statistical question that anticipates variability and names a population.

steps.title: Calculate the unfamiliar quantity
steps.instruction: The observed values are 14, 18, 22, 18 minutes. Calculate their mean.
steps.why: A numerical result is not complete until its unit, assumptions, scale, and tolerance survive a reasonableness check.

content.text: Add all 4 observations and divide by 4; retain the time unit.

content.title: Before calculating
content.text: Predict the sign and approximate magnitude. Enter the requested quantity with a unit, then compare it with that prediction.

hints: Add all 4 observations and divide by 4; retain the time unit.
Keep full working precision and round only the final reported value.
The accepted result is within 0.001 of the exact value and includes minutes or min.

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Data Claims applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Identify misleading scales, omitted groups, confounding, and correlation-causation errors.

checks.description: The evidence cycle runs from problem framing through changed-case validation.
checks.failureMessage: Frame the decision before modeling, and validate after obtaining a result.

checks.description: The decision applies Statistical Question to the changed stakeholder case.
checks.failureMessage: Recheck the conditions and reject the documented misconception about Statistical Question.

checks.description: The decision applies Data Types Sampling to the changed stakeholder case.
checks.failureMessage: Recheck the conditions and reject the documented misconception about Data Types Sampling.

checks.failureMessage: Recheck the conditions and reject the documented misconception about Center Spread.

checks.description: The decision applies Data Claims to the changed stakeholder case.
checks.failureMessage: Recheck the conditions and reject the documented misconception about Data Claims.

checks.description: The calculated quantity is accurate to the stated tolerance and carries its requested unit.
checks.failureMessage: Recalculate from the documented model, check the scale, and include the requested unit.

checks.description: The evidence note explains the model, failure risk, and verification in the learner’s own words.
checks.failureMessage: Add a causal explanation using misleading and scales plus specific case evidence.
